questions.question: Machine learning is a subset of what?
questions.explanation: ML is a subset of AI in which computers learn from data and improve without explicit programming. Deep learning is a further subset of ML using neural networks.

questions.question: Which ML type is trained on labeled data to make predictions or classifications?
questions.explanation: Supervised learning trains on labeled data (each sample has a correct answer attached) to make predictions or classifications on new data.

questions.question: Which ML type discovers patterns and clusters from unlabeled data?
questions.explanation: Unsupervised learning uses unlabeled data and discovers its own patterns, clusters, and relationships. No correct answers are attached to training samples.

questions.question: Which ML type uses an agent that is rewarded or penalized for its actions?
questions.explanation: Reinforcement learning uses an agent that interacts with an environment and receives rewards or penalties. Used in game AIs and self-driving cars.

questions.question: What is deep learning?
options: Learning that takes a long time
ML using multi-layered artificial neural networks for large, complex datasets
questions.explanation: Deep learning is an ML subset that uses multi-layered artificial neural networks to handle large, complex datasets. Its interpretability problem is called 'black box'.

questions.question: What is a hallucination in the context of LLMs?
options: When an LLM makes up an answer that is totally incorrect
questions.explanation: Hallucination is when an LLM makes up an answer that is totally incorrect. It's one of the risks of generative AI.

questions.question: Which Catalyst Center feature performs AI-driven root-cause analysis?
questions.explanation: MRE (Machine Reasoning Engine) performs AI-driven root-cause analysis in Catalyst Center and can take automated corrective actions.

questions.question: Which Catalyst Center feature uses ML to establish a baseline of 'normal' network behavior?
questions.explanation: AI Network Analytics is the umbrella feature in Catalyst Center that uses ML to baseline normal behavior so it can flag anomalies.

questions.question: What does AI-enhanced RRM do?
options: Identifies and classifies devices connecting to the network
Optimizes Wi-Fi via dynamic channel, power, and load balancing on APs
questions.explanation: AI-enhanced RRM (Radio Resource Management) optimizes Wi-Fi by dynamically tuning channels, power, and load balancing across APs.

questions.question: Which is an example of predictive AI?
options: Generating a new image from a prompt
questions.explanation: Predictive AI uses ML on historical data to forecast future outcomes — anomaly detection, traffic forecasting, and predictive maintenance are classic examples.

questions.question: Which is an example of generative AI?
questions.explanation: Generative AI (GenAI) learns patterns from existing data and creates new content. LLMs like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Copilot are examples, as are Midjourney and Sora.

questions.question: What does LLM stand for?
questions.explanation: LLM = Large Language Model — generative text AIs like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Copilot.

questions.question: What does AI Endpoint Analytics do in Catalyst Center?
options: Identify, classify, and profile devices connecting to the network
questions.explanation: AI Endpoint Analytics identifies, classifies, and profiles devices connecting to the network — useful for policy and visibility.
